GOALPOST AT FOOTBALL STADIUM BLOWN OVER.
REPORTED AT WENDOVER POINT OF ENTRY
TREES DOWN IN TREMONTON. WINDOWS BLOWN OUT IN VEHICLES.
LIGHT POLE FELL ON 2 CARS AT SALT LAKE INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT
MULTIPLE TREES DOWN IN THE OGDEN AREA.
MULTIPLE TREES DOWN. POWERLINES REPORTED DOWN ACROSS THE COUNTY.
LARGE TREES DOWN IN ROSE PARK
A PARKED SMALL PRIVATELY OWNED AIR PLANE WAS FLIPPED OVER BY THE STRONG WINDS AT THE BOUNTIFUL AIRPORT.
18-24 INCH DIAMETER TREE SNAPPED AND LANDED ON HOUSE CAUSING MINOR ROOF DAMAGE IN THE AVENUES
AT LEAST TWO DOZEN LARGE TREES DOWN IN HUNTSVILLE. SEVERAL OF THE DOWN TREES DAMAGED STRUCTURES WITH ONE HOUSE WITH MODERATE DAMAGE. MANY POWERLINES DOWN.
PARLEYS CANYON AT QUARRY, UT - UTQRY
REPORT RECEIVED VIA NSSL-NWS SEVERE HAZARDS ANALYSIS AND VERIFICATION PROJECT
FAA SENSOR AT THE SALT LAKE CITY INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT.
SALT LAKE CITY INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT ASOS.
SR-12 AT BOULDER SUMMIT SENSOR, UTBLD.
A RUSSIAN OLIVE TREE WAS KNOCKED OVER AT THE NORTH SIDE OF THE SALT LAKE CITY INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT AND FELL ON A BUILDING. REPORTED BY AIRPORT OPERATIONS.
THE RICH COUNTY SHERIFF REPORTED THAT A LARGE TREE WAS KNOCKED OVER IN WOODRUFF.
LAKE POINT AT I-80 SENSOR, UT9.
A LARGE BARN WAS KNOCKED DOWN AND SEVERAL TREES WERE REPORTED DOWN ACROSS THE AREA.
FLASH FLOOD AFFECTING SULPHUR CREEK AND THE FREMONT RIVER
WATER FLOWING OVER THE CAPITOL REEF NATIONAL PARK SCENIC DRIVE FROM THE VISITORS CENTER TO THE SLICKROCK DIVIDE AREA. TIME OF EVENT IS ESTIMATED.
SULPHUR CREEK IS EXPERIENCING A FLASH FLOOD. THE SCENIC DRIVE HAS BEEN CLOSED.

FLASH FLOOD REPORTED ALONG THE NORTH FORK OF THE VIRGIN. AREAS AFFECTED WERE THE TEMPLE OF SINAWAVA, AND REFIGERATOR CANYON, WHICH IS WHERE THE HIKE TO ANGELS LANDING BEGINS. ADDITIONALLY, THE RIVER GAGE ALONG THE NORTH FORK AT SPRINGDALE-NFVU1-JUMPED FROM 103CFS TO 243CFS IN 15 MINUTES.

FLOODING REPORTED IN CAPITOL REEF NATIONAL PARK. SCENIC DRIVE WAS CLOSED, WITH SIX TO EIGHT INCHES OF WATER FLOWING OVER THE ROAD. GRAND WASH EXPERIENCED FLASH FLOODING, WHICH ALSO IMPACTED SCENIC DRIVE. SULPHUR CREEK ALSO EXPERIENCED FLASH FLOODING.
WATER MAKING ROADS IMPASSABLE AT INTERSECTIONS OF 4000 W/5400 S AND BANGERTER HIGHWAY/5400 S. WATER IS UP TO THE BOTTOMS OF CAR DOORS ON AT LEAST ONE INTERSECTIONS.
AT LEAST HALF A DOZEN HOMES HAVE FLOODED BASEMENTS NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF 5400 S AND 3200 W.
FLASH FLOODING IN NORTH WASH NEAR HOG SPRINGS.
VERY LARGE FLASH FLOOD FROM MULTIPLE DRAINAGES IN THE HENRY MOUNTAINS. DAMAGE TO SR 95 AND SR24.
ADDITIONAL FLASH FLOODING FROM HENRY MOUNTAINS DAMAGING MULTIPLE ROADWAYS IN THE AREA.
FLASH FLOOD ON POISON SPRING WASH. WATER RAN OVER HIGHWAY 95 WHICH MADE IT BRIEFLY IMPASSABLE.
VISITOR REPORTED A FLASH FLOOD BETWEEN MOUNTAIN OF THE SUN AND THE TWIN BROTHERS IN ZION NATIONAL PARK AT APPROXIMATELY 1 PM. FLASH FLOOD WAS VERY SHORT LIVED.
FLASH FLOOD IN CAPITOL REEF NATIONAL PARK IN PLEASANT AND BURRO WASHES.
FLASH FLOOD ALONG THE WEST SLOPE OF THE HENRY MOUNTAINS, SPECIFICALLY SANDY CREEK.
FLASH FLOOD IN THE CATHEDRAL VALLEY OF CAPITOL REEF NP.
CROTON CANYON EXPERIENCED FLASH FLOOD, WASHING OUT THE CROTON CANYON ROAD.
SULPHER CREEK IN CAPITOL REEF NATIONAL PARK FLASH FLOOD.
FLASH FLOOD IN ALVEY AND HARRIS WASHES AS THEY FLOW INTO THE ESCALANTE RIVER
FLASH FLOOD REPORTED ALONG CLEAR CREEK NEAR THE FREMONT INDIAN STATE PARK. CLEAR CREEK ROAD CLOSED DUE DEBRIS.
I-15 AT SEVIER RIVER NORTHBOUND UDOT SENSOR

FLASH FLOODING OCCURRED IN GRAND WASH IN CAPITOL REEF NATIONAL PARK...AND WATER WENT OVER HIGHWAY 24 WHERE THE GRAND WASH MEETS THE ROAD. IN ADDITION...PARK RANGERS REPORTED FLASH FLOODING ALONG THE FREMONT RIVER.
EMERY COUNTY DISPATCH REPORTED QUARTER SIZED HAIL IN HUNTINGTON.
FLASH FLOODING IN CAPITOL REEF NATIONAL PARK JUST SOUTH OF THE VISITORS CENTER. RADAR RAINFALL ESTIMATES INDICATED NEARLY AN INCH FELL IN ABOUT 3 HOURS TIME.
FLASH FLOOD IN SULPHER CREEK. WATER WAS 4-5 FT DEEP AT THE VISITOR CENTER.
WATER AND DEBRIS ACROSS STATE ROUTE 9 BETWEEN MILE POST 22 AND 24.
PARK RANGERS REPORT FLASH FLOODING ALONG PINE CREEK FEEDING INTO CLEAR CREEK OF 7 TO 8 FEET IN DEPTH.

PARK RANGERS REPORTED FLASH FLOODING ALONG PINE CREEK 7 TO 8 FEET DEEP...AND ALONG THE EAST FORK OF THE VIRGIN RIVER 3 TO 4 FEET DEEP. ADDITIONALLY NUMEROUS WATERFALLS HAVE FORMED THROUGHOUT ZION CANYON.
FORT PEARCE WASH GAGE NEAR ST GEORGE - FPWU1
RELAYED BY HIKERS WHO WERE CAUGHT IN CRACK CANYON

LARGE FLASH FLOOD IN CRACK AND LITTLE WILDHORSE CANYONS. HIKERS IN CRACK CANYON RACED OUT OF CANYON DURING STORM AS RUNOFF POURED OVER CANYON WALLS. CAR WAS HIT WITH WATER HALF WAY UP THE DOOR AS THEY CROSSED WASH EXITING THE AREA. GOBLIN VALLEY PARK RANGERS ALSO REPORTED FLOODING ALONG WASHES NEAR LITTLE WILDHORSE CANYON.

WIDESPREAD FLASH FLOODING ACROSS MULTIPLE DRAINAGES IN ZION NP. PINE CREEK FLASHED TO 6-7 FT. SPRY CANYON FLASHED IN ADDITION TO ROCKFALL ACROSS MANY ROADS...AND FLASH FLOODING ACROSS ALL NORTHERN ASPECTS OF ZION NP.
WIDESPREAD FLASH FLOODING ACROSS THE SOUTHWESTERN PART OF ZION NATIONAL PARK. RAINFALL AMOUNTS OF 1.60 INCHES FELL IN A TWO HOUR PERIOD. FLOODING...ROCKFALL...AND LARGER WASHES IN FLOOD.

NATIONAL PARK SERVICE IN CAPITAL REEF REPORTED FLASH FLOODING IN THREE PLACES ACROSS WAYNE COUNTY SOUTHEAST OF TORREY. SULPHUR CREEK THAT RUNS INTO THE FREEMONT RIVER HAD WATER 3 FEET DEEP IN THE CHANNEL. SHEETS GULCH AND DOG WATER CREEK WERE ALSO REPORTED RUNNING WITH LESS THAN A FOOT OF WATER ON THE ROAD. THE NOTOM ROAD WAS NOT DRIVEABLE THIS MORNING.
